root_text.iti107:2.1: “Bahukārā, bhikkhave, brāhmaṇagahapatikā tumhākaṁ ye vo paccupaṭṭhitā cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānapaccayabhesajjaparikkhārehi.
root_text.iti107:2.2: Tumhepi, bhikkhave, bahukārā brāhmaṇagahapatikānaṁ yaṁ nesaṁ dhammaṁ desetha ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāsetha.
root_text.iti107:2.3: Evamidaṁ, bhikkhave, aññamaññaṁ nissāya brahmacariyaṁ vussati oghassa nittharaṇatthāya sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā”ti.
root_text.iti107:7.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti107:0.3: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching
translation_text.iti107:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti107:2.1: “Mendicants, brahmins and householders are very helpful to you, as they provide you with robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.iti107:2.2: And you are very helpful to brahmins and householders, as you teach them the Dhamma that’s good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And you reveal a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.
translation_text.iti107:2.3: That is how this spiritual path is lived supporting each other in order to cross over the flood and make a complete end of suffering.”
translation_text.iti107:3.3: find success in the true teaching,
translation_text.iti107:3.4: the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.iti107:5.3: place faith in the perfected ones,
translation_text.iti107:6.2: the path that goes to a good place,
translation_text.iti107:6.3: they delight in the heavenly realm,
translation_text.iti107:6.4: enjoying all the pleasures they desire.”
translation_text.iti107:7.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
